videos.title: Oil Flows Through Hormuz, Fed Remembers Alan Greenspan | Bloomberg Businessweek Daily 6/22/2026
videos.description:

Then, Jennifer Welch, chief geoeconomics analyst at Bloomberg Economics, discusses the status of talks between the US and Iran as oil begins to flow through the Strait of Hormuz. videos.title: Why the U.K. Can’t Keep a Prime Minister | WSJ
videos.description:
The U.K. is on the cusp of having its sixth leader in just seven years after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. It comes after Starmer – a former human rights lawyer who returned the Labour Party to power for the first time in 14 years – failed to enact change fast enough for a weary British public and faced growing rebellion from his own lawmakers.

WSJ explains what went wrong in Starmer’s premiership and what comes next for the U.K.

videos.title: How Ukraine and Russia Treat Their Prisoners of War Differently | WSJ
videos.description:
Chernihiv, Ukraine — After almost two years in a Russian prison, Ukrainian soldier Oleksandr “Sasha” Kenev was exchanged in a prisoner swap. As he stepped off the bus onto home soil, his wife Khrystyna was there to meet him. What followed were weeks of rehab and therapy as Sasha tried to readjust to life back home in Ukraine. 

WSJ follows Sasha’s journey, and takes a look at what life is like for those who are still waiting for their loved ones to be exchanged.

videos.title: Starmer Quits, Sets Out Plan for New UK PM by September
videos.description:
Keir Starmer said he would step down as Britain’s prime minister after suffering a precipitous fall from favor, paving the way for Andy Burnham to succeed him. On Monday, Burnham, the former mayor of Manchester, said on X that he would “put himself forward.”

Starmer announced on Monday morning that he would step down first as Labour leader, with nominations for a successor opening July 9 and any contest wrapped up by the end of Parliament’s summer break on Sept. 1. He said he would remain in office until the process is done, and “ensure an orderly handover of power.” 

For more, we speak with John Micklethwait, Editor-in-Chief for Bloomberg News.

videos.title: Why ‘Toy Story’ Still Works After 30 Years | WSJ
videos.description:
When Andrew Stanton was hired in 1990 as Pixar’s ninth employee and second animator, he had never made a movie. Today, he’s written or directed ten of the company’s most beloved animated films, including the “Toy Story” franchise, “Finding Nemo,” and “WALL-E.” His latest film – “Toy Story 5” – explores what happens when the toys audiences have known for decades encounter a new threat in the form of a frog-themed tablet. 

WSJ’s Ben Fritz sat down with Stanton to discuss his illustrious career, Pixar’s unique approach to filmmaking, and why their movies are so good at making you cry. “Toy Story 5” starts showing in theaters on Friday, June 19th.

videos.title: How Cheap Drones Are Fueling Colombia’s War | WSJ
videos.description:
Cúcuta, COLOMBIA – Cocaine-funded militias are running a deadly drone war across Colombia, terrorizing civilians and authorities.

Colombia is now the epicenter of unmanned aerial vehicle warfare in the Americas. Weaponised drone attacks by armed groups here now outnumber those anywhere else in the region, including Mexico, where criminal groups were first recorded using the weapons in 2021, according to the conflict monitoring group ACLED.

WSJ spoke with a mother who was injured in what she says was a drone strike that accidentally hit her home and went inside a state-run drone factory, where authorities are scrambling to advance their technology to hit back at criminal armed groups.

videos.title: Daron Acemoglu on the Good-Jobs Imperative — ABCDE 2026
videos.description:
The 2026 Annual Bank Conference on Development Economics (ABCDE) opens with a keynote on on the urgent need for good jobs across the world, featuring World Bank Group Chief Economist Indermit Gill and Nobel laureate Daron Acemoglu.

Over the next 25 years, countries across Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and the Middle East will see an unprecedented surge in their working-age population. This shift could become one of the defining economic opportunities of the century—or a source of growing instability.

World Bank Group Chief Economist Indermit Gill will set the stage for this important discussion, outlining the challenges and opportunities the world will face in creating productive and rewarding jobs for the next generation.

videos.title: Can AI Compute Become The Next Big Futures Market?
videos.description:
For decades, companies have turned to futures markets to manage uncertainty. Airlines hedge fuel costs. Farmers hedge crops. Manufacturers hedge metals. Now a startup wants to bring that same financial machinery to artificial intelligence. Silicon Data and CME Group are working on futures contracts tied to GPU rental prices, a market that could help firms hedge rising AI infrastructure, cloud computing and data center costs. AI companies spend heavily to rent high-end GPU power for training and running artificial intelligence models, but future compute prices can be hard to predict.  CNBC's Yun Li reports.

videos.title: World Bank Group Youth Summit Pitch Competition 2026 – Finals
videos.description:
Join us for the Pitch Competition Finals, where selected young entrepreneurs present their ideas at the World Bank Group Youth Summit. The competition brings together participants aged 18–35 from around the world, working on solutions to development challenges, including job creation, education and skills, and entrepreneurship.

During this session, finalists will pitch their projects and share how their ideas can move from concept to implementation, with a focus on real-world impact.

↘️ Learn more about the six projects in competition:

• Crustea Indonesia, the company behind Eco-Aerator, is advancing sustainable aquaculture through an AI- and IoT-enabled aeration system that helps shrimp farmers monitor water quality in real time, automate oxygen control, reduce energy costs, and improve productivity. [Winner of the Early Ventures Award]

• Bionic Soul is making mobility more accessible by developing high-quality and more affordable lower-limb prosthetics for underserved communities, with a focus on the MENA region and Africa.

• eAgro Pvt Ltd is bringing climate-smart agricultural intelligence to smallholder farmers through AI advisory tools that work even without reliable internet, helping farmers improve yields, reduce climate risks, and increase income.

• PanFa Technologies is using satellite data and machine learning to scale environmentally friendly rice cultivation, helping farmers reduce methane emissions, save water, and unlock carbon-credit revenue. [Winner of the Ideas Award]

• SankofaFresh is building an AI-powered cold chain platform for smallholder farmers in Ghana, combining affordable cold storage, digital market access, and smart logistics to reduce post-harvest losses and improve farmer livelihoods. [Winner of the Audience Choice Award]

• Lesto Circular is turning idle equipment and materials into jobs and circular opportunities by digitizing underused assets and connecting them with entrepreneurs and SMEs that need affordable tools to grow.

Learn more: https://live.worldbank.org/en/event/2026/world-bank-group-youth-summit

videos.title: High-Level Plenary: Development in FCV in a Shifting International Order | Fragility Forum 2026
videos.description:
The World Bank Group Fragility Forum 2026 (June 8-10, 2026) brought together global leaders to align on priorities in an increasingly complex fragility, conflict, and violence (FCV) landscape.

This high-level plenary session was streamed publicly on WB Live, bringing together government officials, development practitioners, and partners to discuss how to deliver lasting impact in fragile and conflict-affected settings.

👤SPEAKERS 
- Mohamed Juldeh Jalloh, Vice President, Republic of Sierra Leone
- Haneen Sayed, Minister of Social Affairs, Lebanon
- Alexandar De Croo, Administrator, @UNDP 
- Birama B. Sidibé, Founder, Agro Biotech
- Ndiame Diop, Vice President for Eastern and Southern Africa, World Bank Group

videos.title: منتدى الهشاشة لمجموعة البنك الدولي: تجديد آليات التعامل مع أوضاع الهشاشة والصراع والعنف
videos.description:
يستهل اليوم الأول من منتدى الهشاشة لمجموعة البنك الدولي لعام 2026 بحوار يضم رئيس مجموعة البنك الدولي أجاي بانغا ورئيس موزمبيق دانيال تشابو، حيث يعرض القائدان طبيعة وأهمية وتحديات العمل التحويلي في أكثر مناطق العالم تعقيدًا. ويتطرقان إلى الأوضاع الراهنة ودور مجموعة البنك الدولي في ظل مشهد سريع التغيّر للهشاشة والنزاع والعنف حول العالم من حيث النهج المعتمد والأساليب المستخدمة في هذه السياقات. وهنا تأتي استراتيجية مجموعة البنك الدولي المحدثة للتعامل مع أوضاع الهشاشة والصراع والعنف المبنية على دروس مستفادة لتكون أكثر فعالية في الإستجابة لهذا المشهد الحالي المتطور.

👤المتحدثون والجدول
(جميع الأوقات بتوقيت الساحل الشرقي للولايات المتحدة)
